title: Blood Circulation
description: Trace the path of a red blood cell as it travels through the heart, lungs, and body.

steps.speech: Your heart is an incredibly powerful double-pump that keeps you alive by circulating blood. Let's trace its journey.
Deoxygenated blood, carrying carbon dioxide from your body, enters the right side of the heart through the Vena Cava.
The right ventricle pumps this blue, oxygen-poor blood out through the pulmonary artery.
It travels straight to the lungs. Here, you exhale carbon dioxide and inhale fresh oxygen, turning the blood bright red!
This oxygen-rich blood returns to the left side of the heart via the pulmonary veins.
Now for the big squeeze! The muscular left ventricle pumps the oxygenated blood out through the Aorta—the largest artery in your body.
The oxygen is delivered to all your body tissues, muscles, and organs. As they use the oxygen, the blood becomes deoxygenated again, and the cycle repeats!
